lede: A look at February 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including David Cecil, 6th Marquess of Exeter and Jimmy Bennett, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Bad Aibling rail accident
events.desc: Two passenger trains collide in the German town of Bad Aibling in the state of Bavaria. Twelve people die and 85 others are injured.

events.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
events.desc: The Provisional Irish Republican Army declares the end to its 18-month ceasefire and explodes a large bomb in London's Canary Wharf, killing two people.

events.title: Soyuz 17
events.desc: The Soyuz 17 Soviet spacecraft returns to Earth.

events.title: 1971 San Fernando earthquake
events.desc: The 6.5–6.7 Mw Sylmar earthquake hits the Greater Los Angeles Area with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), killing 64 and injuring 2,000.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Bombing of Genoa: The Cathedral of San Lorenzo in Genoa, Italy, is struck by a bomb which fails to detonate.

events.title: Brazil
events.desc: Brazil becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty.

events.title: Svalbard Treaty
events.desc: Under the terms of the Svalbard Treaty, international diplomacy recognizes Norwegian sovereignty over Arctic archipelago Svalbard, and designates it as demilitarized.

events.title: Mud March (suffragists)
events.desc: The Mud March is the first large procession organised by the National Union of Women's Suffrage Societies (NUWSS).

events.title: United States Electoral College
events.desc: After no candidate receives a majority of electoral votes in the US presidential election of 1824, the United States House of Representatives elects John Quincy Adams as sixth President of the United States in a contingent election.

events.title: Haiti
events.desc: Haiti attacks the newly established Dominican Republic on the other side of the island of Hispaniola.

births.name: William Henry Harrison
births.line: American general and politician, 9th President of the United States

births.name: Joseph Stiglitz
births.line: American economist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Ernst von Dohnányi, Hungarian pianist, composer, and conductor
Fyodor Dostoevsky, Russian novelist, short story writer, essayist, and philosopher
William I, Margrave of Meissen, Margrave of Meissen
